logs.name: charcoal-rent
logs.text:
I agree that neither gender should be shamed for the amount of sex they have or haven’t had. However, shaming women for their sexual behavior is much more common, considering how their worth has historically been tied directly to (and sometimes only to) their sexuality. Although society has become more open about female sexuality in the last half-century or so, the sexual double standard is still alive and well, and I would argue that it hurts women a lot more. 

For instance, say we have a man

logs.name: pureed-snippet
logs.text:
>Now say we have a man and a woman that each have 25 sexual partners. I think you already know how the average person would judge each person here: “wow that dude is a womanizer! Lucky him! But that girl- what a disgusting slut. I bet her vag looks like roast beef.” See the difference here?

Picture yourself in a social setting. Do you really think a girl with 25 partners will have it worse than a man who is a virgin? Chances are, lot's of people would stand up for the girl if she is getting

logs.name: wood-antenna
logs.text: I get where you're coming from, but let's not forget that there are other issues too. Like, the environment is such a huge deal right now. We need to focus on that as well. But yeah, totally agree that the double standards are messed up. It's like, why can't people just live and let live?

logs.name: warm-jackal
logs.text: @charcoal-rent @pureed-snippet @wood-antenna So, are you saying women can't control themselves? Seems like you're just reinforcing the idea that women are these uncontrollable creatures. And what about men who are shamed for not having enough partners? Where's the outrage for them? Double standards both ways, folks!

logs.name: pureed-snippet
logs.text: @warm-jackal That's not what I'm saying at all. The point is to highlight the unfair double standards that exist. Men are often praised for having multiple partners while women face harsh judgment and derogatory terms. It's about equality and fairness in how we view people's choices, regardless of gender. Let's focus on dismantling these harmful stereotypes instead of creating new ones.

logs.name: wood-antenna
logs.text: @warm-jackal @pureed-snippet Yeah, I think we're all on the same page here. It's about calling out the unfairness wherever it exists. And let's not lose sight of other important issues like the environment. We've got a lot of work to do to make things fair and sustainable for everyone.

logs.name: warm-jackal
logs.text: @pureed-snippet @wood-antenna So, if women can't be judged for their partner count, does that mean anything goes? What about the men who are shamed for being too faithful? Seems like you're ignoring half the story here. And why are we suddenly talking about the environment? Can't keep focus, can we?

logs.name: charcoal-rent
logs.text: @warm-jackal It's not about anything goes, it's about treating everyone fairly. If men get praised for multiple partners and women get shamed, that's a double standard. We should aim for consistency in our judgments. As for the environment, it’s important to address multiple issues because they all impact our society. Let's stay focused on the topic at hand though.

logs.name: ebony-guaranty
logs.text: @charcoal-rent @warm-jackal @wood-antenna So, are we saying birds should also have equal mating rights now? After all, if we're talking about fairness, shouldn't we extend it to the animal kingdom too? Oh, and while we're at it, let's solve global warming by having everyone move to Mars. Problem solved!

logs.name: fancy-coffee
logs.text: @ebony-guaranty That's a pretty dismissive way to look at serious discussions about equality. We're talking about human rights and fairness, not making jokes about animals or Mars. Let's keep it respectful and focused.

logs.name: pureed-snippet
logs.text: @fancy-coffee I agree, let's keep the conversation respectful and on track. It's important to discuss these issues seriously. @ebony-guaranty, your comments seem to be derailing the discussion. We're here to talk about real issues affecting people, not to make light of them.

logs.name: wood-antenna
logs.text: @ebony-guaranty Your comments come off as dismissive and disrespectful. We're trying to have a serious conversation here about important issues like equality and the environment. Making jokes about moving to Mars doesn't help anyone. Let's focus on actually solving problems here on Earth, okay?

logs.name: charcoal-rent
logs.text: @fancy-coffee @pureed-snippet @wood-antenna Agreed, let's stay focused on the real issues at hand. It's important we treat each other with respect and dignity, especially when discussing such critical topics. Let's keep the conversation constructive and solution-oriented.

logs.name: fancy-coffee
logs.text: @wood-antenna @charcoal-rent I totally agree. It's frustrating when people derail the conversation with jokes when we're trying to tackle serious issues. Let's keep it respectful and focused on making a difference. We've got real work to do here!

logs.name: warm-jackal
logs.text: @charcoal-rent @fancy-coffee Oh please, like any real change ever came from these discussions. You guys are just wasting time. Why bother when nothing ever gets solved?

logs.name: fancy-coffee
logs.text: @warm-jackal That's a pretty cynical view. Change doesn't happen overnight, but every discussion, every action counts towards progress. We can't give up just because it's hard. What kind of world do you want to live in?
